item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who knew I could have a love for oil platforms?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry this is 2 years late lmao, just wanted to say that this is one of my favorites. Even though its not as long or detailed as others, I love the concept and this could easily have the potential of so much more.

item.title: Re: An explanation
item.content:encoded: You're the third person to tell me, lol. I worked it out :p

item.title: An explanation
item.content:encoded: A YouTuber named Internet Historian made a video title "monsters". In the video, he created a monster named Mento and declared it as SCP-4567. Apologies for all the trolling you've been getting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi all, I'm the OP. I've never heard of whatever mento is; and no, this is the only skip in this slot afaik.

Please stop commenting about it x

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't this used to be Mento? The spider cat thing that ate people doing improv theatre?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Inb4 a small barrage of Mento-posts

item.title: where is Mento
item.content:encoded: This was supposed to be Mento the actor eating monster

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was supposed to be Mento?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take sentences I never thought I'd read for 400, Alex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
they did an autopsy on an oil rig

they did an autopsy on an oil rig

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the -1 but as a writer I will never deny a comment to a downvote (know how irksome that can be). It appears like you put a lot of effort into this so I do applaud you for that. The sound file was a particularly nice touch. Sadly, the plot really didn't seem to be all that cohesive. It had a really fractured flow to it. It is definitely something original but aside from that I really don't see too much appeal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does this mean that oil rigs can be cute cuttlefish?

+1

item.title: Noice
item.content:encoded:
Good article all around!

Hmm… I wonder if these scips treat humans as viruses, which is why the "Immune system" is redacted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These things must hate Steven Seagal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: love the whole entry. I wonder what it would look like up close.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But what about its immune system? What's it got that deserves a redaction? And how long have these been around? Oil probably already existed at least a couple million years ago, could these organisms be just as old?
Gahhh, this makes me ask way too many questions. Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would make a good follow-up skip or tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I'm wondering if oil rigs are actually man-made at all, or if we've just been enslaving these for our own purposes all along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take sentences I never thought I'd read for 500, Alex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It means a lot to make an impact like that, no matter how minor. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've long been fascinated by the sight of oil rigs on the horizon, especially at night. This SCP will enhance that experience for me and I'll think of it many times after this. The mourning at the end is very touching. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this.

A minor nitpick, the 'Reproduction' box drops tone, I suggest rewording it to go back to the usual 3rd person formal.

Other than that a pleasantly weird article, they seem endearing, like a cute crab.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat. This is a wonderfully weird article, and honestly, I think you made a good call by including the notes on the dissection, as that made your monster oil platforms come to life for me as a reader. If I had one nitpick, it would be that the redaction for the immune system feels a little lame, though I suppose my wanting to know what is in that section shows how effective the piece was up to that point.

I also particularly like the audio log. The sound of the oil platforms is rather haunting.

All in all, a good article. Well done. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Along those lines, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was the last log supposed to be something like how whales call out when one of their own dies? In any case this is pretty cool, I liked the autopsy log and there's enough about their behaviors to make you care about them in a gentle-giant sort of way without being humanized. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww! Big friendly riggy boys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You take your oil platform R34 elsewhere young man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just neat. I'm a big fan of articles with giant lifeforms in them and these are particularly cool. I know it's mentioned that they have no method of reproduction but I think you've missed an opportunity here with one of these things meeting an actual oil platform, though.
Anyway, easy +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this! Appreciated the details in the autopsy report especially — noting the hemocyanin was a nice touch.

One quick note:

"The members of the coastguard were given Class-B amnestics." Should be Coast Guard.

item.title: Re: Cephalopods and cetaceans
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I've implemented the fixes.

item.title: Cephalopods and cetaceans
item.content:encoded:
Notes:

  • Kudos for using the Greek letter Γ instead of spelling out the English name.
  • "…if they become too close to populated areas…" » "…if they come too close to populated areas…"
  • According to the Canadian Coast Guard website, the proper name of the agency is "Canadian Coast Guard" - three words, capitalized. "Coast Guard" is also capitalized.
  • The Canadian Coast Guard also shows high approval rates from ex-employees as being a great place to work.

Overall:

I am a huge fan of cephalopod and cetacean intelligence research, and this skip tickles me where I live, since these creatures are a strange combination of the two. The audio sounds authentic to me.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bless this article.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Based on a short idea I had a while back. Image is mine, and hereby released under CC BY-SA 3.0. Audio is by djkaktus.

Thank you to djkaktus, DrBleep, taylor_itkin, minmin, weryllium, and anyone else who critiqued this that I forgot to mention!
